message:
feat(core): reactive dataflow graph and sharded mux routing modules

Add two new foundational modules to frankenterm-core that support
declarative agent orchestration and multi-mux deployment topologies.

dataflow.rs (1277 lines):
Reactive dataflow graph engine inspired by Jane Street's Incremental
and Salsa frameworks. Provides a directed acyclic graph (DAG) where
nodes hold typed values connected by dependency edges. When upstream
values change, downstream dependents are lazily invalidated and
recomputed on demand. Key components:
  - NodeId/EdgeId handle types for graph addressing
  - DataflowGraph<T> with topological-sort propagation
  - Memoized recomputation that skips clean subtrees
  - Cycle detection via DFS coloring during propagation
  - Batch update coalescing for bulk mutations
  - Observer registration for external change notification
  - Graph statistics and diagnostic instrumentation

sharding.rs (1053 lines):
Sharded WezTerm routing layer for multi-mux deployments where agent
fleets may span multiple WezTerm server instances. Provides consistent
hash-ring based shard assignment with virtual nodes for load balancing.
Key components:
  - ShardId and ShardKey types for routing identity
  - ConsistentHashRing with configurable virtual node count
  - ShardRouter that maps pane/tab/workspace keys to mux endpoints
  - Rebalancing logic for shard addition/removal with minimal churn
  - Health-aware routing that excludes unhealthy shards
  - Locality hints for co-locating related panes on the same shard

Supporting files:
  - proptest_dataflow.rs: 340 lines of property-based tests covering
    graph invariants, propagation correctness, cycle rejection, batch
    semantics, and observer notification guarantees
  - proptest_sharding.rs: 162 lines of property-based tests verifying
    consistent hashing stability, rebalance churn bounds, and
    health-aware routing fallback behavior
  - shard_routing.rs: 274-line Criterion benchmark suite measuring
    routing throughput for 10/100/1000-shard rings, rebalance cost,
    and health-check overhead with CI budget annotations
  - lib.rs: registers both new public modules

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
